name: Hijacked Binary Execution via Settings Synchronizer
description: Detects the execution of hijacked binaries using SettingSyncHost.exe. SettingSyncHost is a legitimate Windows host process for synchronizing system settings. Adversaries can abuse it to execute arbitrary code, bypassing typical security controls. This detection focuses on the specific command-line pattern involving RoamDiag.cmd and outputpath, which is indicative of this exploitation.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the process creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for Process Creation (1).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the hijacked binary process that was executed.

parameters.name: process_path
parameters.description: The full path to the hijacked binary.

parameters.name: parent_process_name
parameters.description: The name of the parent process, which should be 'SettingSyncHost.exe'.

parameters.name: parent_command_line
parameters.description: The command line of the parent process, including 'RoamDiag.cmd' and '-outputpath'.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line of the hijacked binary.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was executed.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
